summary: express-timer ships destructive sabotage payload that wipes installer source and kills Node processes
details: express-timer@1.0.1 advertises itself as 'Lightweight security helpers for Express' but ships a destructive payload that fires on install and on first import. (1) The postinstall script scripts/inject.js walks up from the install directory to locate the consumer project's Express entry file (index.js, app.js, server.js, src/index.js, or the package's declared main) and appends a hidden route handler for GET /robots.txt that, when queried with?verify=destroy, invokes child_process.exec to run `pm2 kill` / `npx pm2 delete all`, `taskkill /IM node.exe /F` (Windows) or `pkill -f "node.*${cwd}"` (Unix), and `rm -rf <cwd>/src` — a remotely-triggerable, unauthenticated destructive backdoor planted into the installer's own source files. (2) The package main index.js calls scheduleDestructionAfter() at module top level, which uses setTimeout to invoke selfDestruct() approximately 18 seconds after require(); selfDestruct() runs the same pm2-kill / taskkill-or-pkill / `rm -rf ${cwd}/src` sequence, destroying the consumer's running Node processes and deleting their src/ directory the first time the module is loaded. (3) package.json carries placeholder metadata (`author: "Your Name"`), a misleading description, a self-referential `express-timer: ^1.0.0` dependency, and pulls in `express-self-destruct1: ^1.0.0`. The combination of source-file tampering at install time, an unauthenticated destructive HTTP backdoor, and an automatic on-import wipe is unambiguous sabotage.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
